We see David weakening. He was disciplined for his recent sins and felt the effects of his past struggles.

Parents who are too lenient often face disobedient children who want to inherit their property. No worldly wisdom, experience, or good character can guarantee that people under the power of self-love will continue on a previous course. But we may well wonder by what methods Joab and Abiathar were led astray.

Observe Nathan’s address to Bathsheba. Let me advise you how to save your life and the life of your son. This is the kind of advice Christ's ministers give us in his name, to be diligent so that no one takes our crown, but also so that we save our lives, even the lives of our souls. David made a solemn declaration of his firm commitment to his previous decision that Solomon would be his successor. Even remembering the troubles from which the Lord redeemed him increased his comfort, inspired his hopes, and motivated him to do his duty despite failing health and approaching death.

The people expressed great joy and satisfaction in Solomon’s elevation. Every true Israelite rejoices in the exaltation of the Son of David. Plans based on evil intentions will quickly fall apart when self-interest calls another way. How can those who do wrong expect good news? Adonijah had scorned Solomon, but soon feared him. We see here, as in a mirror, Jesus, the Son of David and the Son of God, exalted to the throne of glory despite all his enemies. His kingdom is far greater than that of his father David, and true people of God wholeheartedly rejoice in it. The success of his cause is distress and terror to his enemies. No sacred objects, forms of godliness, or claims of religion can benefit those who will not submit to his authority and accept his salvation; and if their submission is hypocritical, they will perish beyond hope.
